title: Tennessee softball ace Karlyn Pickens leaves Belmont game with injury
fullText: Tennessee softball ace Karlyn Pickens left the game against Belmont with an apparent injury in the second inning.The senior pitcher, who started the midweek matchup, grabbed her right shoulder immediately after throwing a pitch in the second at-bat of the inning. Pickens didn't appear to be in a lot visible pain, but she immediately called for a timeout and for pitching coach Megan Rhodes Smith to come out to the circle, along with the athletic trainer.AdvertisementAfter talking with coaches and attempting to throw another pitch, Pickens grimaced, shaking her head and left the game.Pickens was relieved by junior Sage Mardjetko. No. 1 Tennessee (19-0) led Belmont 1-0 when Pickens had to leave the game. Pickens had two strikeouts, a walk and no hits allowed in 1⅓ innings.Pickens (6-0) is the reigning back-to-back SEC Pitcher of the Year. The two-time first-team All-American has a 0.62 ERA with 68 strikeouts on the season. She's coming off a junior season that she ended with a 1.17 ERA, 306 strikeouts in 226⅔ innings with seven shutouts as she led Tennessee to the Women's College World Series semifinals.Cora Hall is the University of Tennessee women’s athletics reporter for Knox News. Email: cora.hall@knoxnews.com; X: @corahalll; Bluesky: @corahall.bsky.social‬. Support strong local journalism and unlock premium perks:knoxnews.com/subscribeThis article originally appeared on Knoxville News Sentinel: Karlyn Pickens leaves Tennessee softball vs Belmont with injury
summary: Tennessee softball senior Karlyn Pickens left the game against Belmont with an apparent injury. Pickens is the Lady Vols' top pitcher on staff.
